back_translation: Paul, the one who has received the (royal-honored) call to be an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will-purpose of God, and Sosthenes, our brother.

To: the church of God which is located in the city of Corinth — that is, those who have already been sanctified in Jesus Christ, who are called to be saints (tham-mik-chon) — together with everyone in every place who calls out the (royal) name of Jesus Christ our Lord, both theirs and ours.
May grace and peace from God our Father, and from Jesus Christ the Lord, abide with all of you.
I give thanks to my God always concerning you, because of the grace of God which was given to you in Jesus Christ.
Because in Him, you have received fullness in every respect — that is, in every aspect of speech and in every aspect of knowledge.
According as the testimony concerning Christ has been confirmed in you.
Resulting in your not lacking any gift, while you are eagerly awaiting the (royal) coming-and-appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ.
He will (royally) make you steadfast until the end, so that you may be without blemish on the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
God is faithful (royal-form). He Himself is the one who has called you into fellowship-communion with His Son, that is, our Lord Jesus Christ.

Brothers and sisters, I beseech all of you in the (royal) name of our Lord Jesus Christ — that you all speak in agreement, that there be no divisions among you, but that you be of one heart-mind together, both in thought and in resolve.
My brothers and sisters, for I have been informed by Chloe's people concerning you that there are quarrels arising among you.
What I mean is this: each one of you is saying, "I am of Paul's faction," "I am of Apollos's faction," "I am of Cephas's faction," "I am of Christ's faction."
Has Christ been (royally) divided? Was Paul crucified on the cross for you? Or were you baptized into the name of Paul?
I give thanks that I did not baptize anyone among you, except Crispus and Gaius.
So that no one would say that you were baptized into my name.
(In fact, I also baptized the household of Stephanas. Beyond that, I do not remember whether I baptized anyone else.)
Because Christ did not (royally) send me to baptize, but (royally) sent me to proclaim the good news — and not by means of the wisdom of speech — so that the cross of Christ would not be made empty.
Because the message concerning the cross is foolishness to those who are perishing, but for us who are receiving salvation, that message is the power of God.
Because there is a written word: "We will destroy the wisdom of those who have wisdom, and we will cause the understanding of those who have understanding to be made empty."
Where is the one with wisdom? Where is the scribe (tham-ma-jarn)? Where is the disputer of this age? Has not God already caused the wisdom of this world to become foolishness?
Because in the wisdom of God, the world could not come to know God through its own wisdom — therefore God was pleased to save those who believe by means of the foolishness of the proclamation.
Because the Jews demand signs, and the Greeks seek wisdom.
But we proclaim Christ the one (royally) crucified — which is a stumbling-block for the Jews, and is foolishness for the Gentiles.
But for those whom He has called, both Jews and Greeks, Christ (royally) is the power of God and the wisdom of God.
Because the foolishness-thing of God is yet wiser than mankind, and the weakness-thing of God is yet stronger than mankind.
All you brothers and sisters, consider the calling that you received: by the standard of the flesh, among you there are not many wise people, not many people of power, not many people of high birth.

But God has (royally) chosen the foolish things of this world, in order to make the wise ashamed; and God has (royally) chosen the weak things of this world, in order to make the strong things ashamed.

And God has (royally) chosen the lowly things in this world, and the despised things — that is, the things that are not anything at all — in order that He might cause the things that are something to become things that are not.
So that no flesh would boast before the (royal) face of God.
But because of Him, you abide in Jesus Christ, the one who has (royally) become wisdom from God for us — being also righteousness, sanctification, and redemption.
So that, just as it is written, "Whoever boasts, let him boast in the Lord."
